CTA-5378

FY26 Unsubscribe Analysis

Generated 2026-07-21 15:53 UTC · FY26 fiscal window Aug 1 2025 – Jul 31 2026 · Source: tax_dm_ca.fact_catax_mktg_event · Published report

How to read this report: We looked at everyone who unsubscribed in FY26 and asked two questions: (1) Which email did they unsubscribe from? (2) Which emails did they receive earlier? When a campaign shows up in many journeys but people rarely unsub on it directly, that's a calendar frequency problem — not just a creative problem on one email.

Executive Summary

  • 11,733 people unsubscribed from email in FY26 (Aug 1 2025 – Jul 31 2026 fiscal year).
  • DSKT (desktop offers) has the highest unsub rate per email delivered (0.20%) in the FY26 volume table.
  • The email most people unsubscribed on: TT121385 (991 unsubs).
  • Promotional emails (PRE) appeared in 72% of unsubscriber journeys — a calendar cadence signal.
  • NETFILE (TT982869) was received by 61% of unsubscribers but only 7.0% unsubscribed on it — reduce sends, not just fix one email.
  • Among unsubscribers who received PRE/POST/ENG/DSKT mail, 39% had ≤3 emails before leaving; 17% had 10+.
  • PRE unsub rate peaks at 3 emails received (0.74% of that 115,874-person cohort). Early PRE exposure is where exit risk concentrates.

FY27 Recommendations

PriorityCampaign / CodeActionWhy
Act nowPRE (promotional)Reduce FY27 sends72% of unsubscribers received PRE (promotional) before leaving, but only 56% unsubscribed on it — likely a calendar frequency issue.
Act nowDSKT (desktop offers)Reduce FY27 sends58% of unsubscribers received DSKT (desktop offers) before leaving, but only 38% unsubscribed on it — likely a calendar frequency issue.
ReviewENG (engagement newsletters)Review creative / sequencingOften the visible unsub button (14% unsubscribed here) — fatigue is likely driven by upstream promotional volume, not this email alone.
MonitorPOST (post-season)Low priority — no calendar changeSaw before leaving: 6%; where they unsubscribed: 0% — monitor and review content relevance.

Priority is based on how often a campaign appears in unsubscriber journeys and unsub rates — not causal proof. Use for FY27 planning; deeper analysis recommended before cutting sends.

Grain: email events (not distinct people). Unsub % = unsubscribed ÷ delivered. Where they unsubbed % and Saw before leaving % are subscriber-grain (can exceed 100% summed across codes) — same framing as the path-to-unsub chart below.

FY26 journey phases — all emails (population)

When unsubs happen across the full FY26 calendar — not just one example subscriber.

Act 1: Welcome Aug–Dec 2025

Pre-season nurture — YRE newsletters and desktop warm-up.

5.5M sent · 5.5M delivered · 3.1M opened · 156K clicked

99% delivered · 56% opened · 5.1% CTOR · 0.03% unsub/delivered

Where they unsubbed: 1,854 (15.8% of all unsubs) · Saw before leaving: 9,598 (81.8% of unsubscribers)

10.6M sent · 10.5M delivered · 5.8M opened · 370K clicked

100% delivered · 55% opened · 6.4% CTOR · 0.02% unsub/delivered

Where they unsubbed: 1,665 (14.2% of all unsubs) · Saw before leaving: 7,610 (64.9% of unsubscribers)

99% delivered · 46% opened · 6.5% CTOR · 0.03% unsub/delivered

Where they unsubbed: 5,505 (46.9% of all unsubs) · Saw before leaving: 8,118 (69.2% of unsubscribers)

Journey Segmentation — How Many Emails Before They Unsubscribed?

Among everyone who received emails from PRE, POST, ENG, or DSKT in FY26 — how does unsubscribe risk change with journey length?

SegmentSubscribers in CohortUnsubbedUnsub Rate %
Short (≤3 emails)58373933470.57
Medium (4–9 emails)90129537590.42
Long (10+ emails)135937314740.11
Unsubscribe rate by journey length (Short / Medium / Long)

Rate = unsubbed ÷ subscribers in cohort. Emails = send events from PRE, POST, ENG, or DSKT.

Short (≤3) (583,739 subscribers)
0.57%
Medium (4–9) (901,295 subscribers)
0.42%
Long (10+) (1,359,373 subscribers)
0.11%

Emails = send events from PRE, POST, ENG, or DSKT (each physical send counted once). Subscribers in cohort = everyone who received that many emails; Unsub Rate % = unsubbed ÷ cohort.

Unsub Rate by How Many Emails They Received

PRE unsub rate peaks at 3 emails (0.74%) — about 1x the rate of people who received just one PRE email (0.68%). Only 115,874 subscribers received exactly 3 PRE emails.

Each chart counts emails from that code only. Rate = unsubscribers ÷ subscribers who received N emails from that code. Highlighted bar is the peak per code.

Where they unsubscribed

Each row is one email send event in this subscriber's FY26 journey (28 distinct sends before exit). Counts toward Saw PRE before leaving (72% population) but not Where they unsubscribed = PRE (56%) — unsub on ENG (TT726227). Opens are directional (Apple MPP); clicks are stronger signal.

Campaigns to Review for FY27 (Top 5)

PriorityCampaignActionWhy
MonitorTT121385 — April-PRE-AprilCountdown-Offers&Promotions-NA-W3-TT1213Low priority — no calendar changeSaw before leaving: 28%; where they unsubscribed: 8% — reduce sends even though another email is often where they click Unsubscribe.
ReviewTT419336 — March-PRE-ImpliedConsent-Transactional-NA-OTS-TT419336Review creative / sequencingHighest unsub rate per delivery (0.78%) among campaign codes — review offer content and targeting.
Act nowTT982869 — March-PRE-NETFILE-Offers&Promotions-TTD-W3&W4-TT982869Reduce FY27 sends61% of unsubscribers received TT982869 (March-PRE-NETFILE-Offers&Promotions-TTD-W3&W4-TT982869) before leaving, but only 7% unsubscribed on it — likely a calendar frequency issue.
MonitorTT121385 — April-PRE-AprilCountdown-Offers&Promotions-NA-W2-TT1213Low priority — no calendar changeSaw before leaving: 28%; where they unsubscribed: 6% — reduce sends even though another email is often where they click Unsubscribe.
ReviewTT393029 — March-PRE-TTDCheckIn-Offers&Promotions-NA-W1&W2-TT39302Review creative / sequencingSaw before leaving: 35%; where they unsubscribed: 6% — reduce sends even though another email is often where they click Unsubscribe.

Among unsubscribers only: how many distinct campaigns each person received before they unsubscribed. Complements the short/medium/long segmentation above.

Appendix: Population engagement (opens & clicks)
  • 11,533 unsubscribers in FY26 window.
  • Median open rate before unsub: 85.7%
  • Median click rate before unsub: 23.1%
  • Opened last-touch email: 84.4%
  • Clicked last-touch email: 98.7%
Appendix: Phase population summary
PhaseEmails SentDeliveredOpenedClickedUnsubs Where They Left% Where UnsubbedSubs Saw Before Leaving% Saw Before Leaving
Act 1: Welcome553691054972963077214156381185415.78959881.69
Act 2: Ramp10563141105189525767843370184166514.17761064.77
Act 3: Sustain15950196158501167290721475850550546.85811869.09
Act 4: Exit54106855392737304906255857272623.20259322.07

Calendar phases match Act 1–4 cards in the path-to-unsub section.

Appendix: Methodology & data notes
  • FY26 window: Intuit fiscal year Aug 1 2025 – Jul 31 2026 (events filtered on mktg_event_datetime; partitions 2024–2025).
  • Dedup: min(mktg_event_datetime) per (campaign_name, campaign_step_name, catax_email_key, event_type).
  • Opens/clicks: per send at same grain; opens are directional (Apple MPP inflates); clicks are stronger.
  • Code predicates: PRE/POST token match on campaign_step_name; ENG = %EmailEngagement%; DSKT = %Desktop%.
  • Where they unsubscribed: campaign on the unsubscribe event (Braze ground truth).
  • Saw before leaving: % of unsubscribers who received at least one send of that code/campaign before unsub.
  • Action tiers: Act now = journey ≥50% or high last-touch + large hidden risk; Review = high rate or journey 30–50%; Monitor = low presence.
  • Cohort unsub rate (Question B): subscriber grain — emails received per code (PRE/POST/ENG/DSKT) or distinct campaigns in legacy/tenure views. Tenure L2 from PAM FY25.
  • Phase population: calendar Act 1–4 volume and unsub timing across all FY26 emails.
  • Email volume totals: count of distinct campaign-step × subscriber events per code (not distinct people).
  • Out of scope: Cox survival, Markov removal effect, causal holdout, ML fatigue scoring.
Draft — inferred campaign attribution. This tab measures a derived full promotional opt-out: the first FY26 transition where a subscriber turned all six consent categories (product, offer, training, content, survey, partner) off. It is rebuilt from consent change history in tax_stg_ca.stg_catax_email_consent and never uses the email_consent_summary column. Because consent records carry no campaign ID, each opt-out is attributed to the latest email delivered in the 90 days before it — a proxy for exposure, not proof of cause. 22% of opt-outs have no qualifying delivery in that window and are shown as Unattributed.

What This Tab Measures

  • 105,594 subscribers completed a full promotional opt-out in FY26 — about 12x the 8,607 who clicked a Braze unsubscribe (3.71% vs 0.30% of the 2,845,735-subscriber mailed cohort).
  • Only 322 subscribers did both — a Braze click rarely equals a full opt-out, so the consent signal surfaces exits the Braze view misses.
  • 78% of full opt-outs (82,671) had a PRE/POST/ENG/DSKT delivery within 90 days and are attributed to a last-touch campaign; the rest are kept as Unattributed.
  • PRE appears before 76% of full opt-outs but is the attributed last touch for only 51% — a calendar-frequency signal, same framing as the Braze tab.
SignalSubscribersRate of cohortWhat it captures
Braze unsubscribe click8,6070.30%Clicked unsubscribe on one email
Derived full promotional opt-out105,5943.71%Transitioned to all six promotional categories off in FY26

Both signals use the same 2,845,735-subscriber FY26 mailed cohort. The Braze tab counts unsubscribe clicks; this tab counts full promotional opt-outs from consent history. The 8,607 Braze figure here is the tagged-cohort subset of Tab 1's 11,733 FY26 Braze unsubscribers (3,126 unsubscribed via untagged mail or email-key/email-string grain); the full step-by-step reconciliation is on the Signal Reconciliation tab. This opt-out count is the FY26-window transition figure (Mode B), not the larger current-state snapshot (Mode A) — see the Method appendix.

How These Numbers Were Derived

Displayed metricSQL definitionQuery outputCalculation
FY26 mailed cohortDistinct emails with ≥1 PRE/POST/ENG/DSKT send, Aug 1 2025–Jul 31 20262,845,735Direct COUNT(DISTINCT email)
Braze unsubscribe rateDistinct cohort emails with event_type = unsubscribe in FY268,6078,607 ÷ 2,845,735 = 0.3025%
Derived full opt-out rateFirst FY26 transition from <6 to all 6 consent categories off105,594105,594 ÷ 2,845,735 = 3.7106%
Attribution coverageLatest tracked delivery strictly before opt-out and within 90 days82,671 attributed82,671 ÷ 105,594 = 78.2914%
Unattributed full opt-outsNo tracked delivery strictly before opt-out within 90 days22,923105,594 − 82,671 = 22,923

Population query: prelim_consent_population.sql · result generated 2026-07-13T21:07:44.917344809+00:00
Attribution query: prelim_consent_attribution.sql · result generated 2026-07-13T21:31:15.505165577+00:00
Dashboard generation is blocked unless all 11 machine-readable reconciliation checks pass.

Tagged Code Exposure Before Full Opt-out

CodeDeliveredAttributed Code-tag CountCode-tag / Delivery %Braze UnsubsAttributed Latest %Saw Before Opt-out %
PRE (promotional)18.2M53,8280.2966,62051.076.3
POST (post-season)1.5M4,8690.33004.614.9
ENG (engagement newsletters)7.1M31,1040.4351,70729.556.8
DSKT (desktop offers)2.3M4,0810.1794,4923.911.2

Campaign steps can carry more than one code tag. Therefore code-tag counts are intentionally non-exclusive and do not sum to the 82,671 unique attributed subscribers: 11,211 subscribers have a multi-code last-touch campaign. Code-tag / Delivery % = code-tag count ÷ delivered email events. Braze Unsubs are shown only for directional comparison.

Which Codes Are in the Path to Full Opt-out?

Campaign codes in the path to full opt-out
Attributed latest exposure Saw before opt-out

A much taller lighter bar indicates repeated journey exposure worth further investigation.

PRE
51.0%
76.3%
POST
4.6%
14.9%
ENG
29.5%
56.8%
DSKT
3.9%
11.2%

Act 1: Welcome Aug–Dec 2025

Pre-season nurture — YRE newsletters and desktop warm-up.

8,601 full opt-outs · 8% of total · 0% attributed

Act 2: Ramp Jan–Feb 2026

Tax season promos begin — HeadStart, NETFILE, Flash Sale.

15,287 full opt-outs · 14% of total · 82% attributed

Act 3: Sustain Mar–Apr 2026

Mid-season cadence — mixed PRE and engagement mail.

54,358 full opt-outs · 51% of total · 81% attributed

Act 4: Exit May–Jul 2026

Peak through late season exit window.

27,348 full opt-outs · 26% of total · 96% attributed

When the lighter bar (saw before opt-out) is much taller than the darker bar (attributed last touch), that is a directional calendar-frequency signal; it does not prove causation.

Partial vs Full Opt-out — Do They Behave Differently?

The small Braze-vs-consent overlap is not noise — it is two different behaviors. Splitting the mailed cohort by how many of the six consent categories are off separates them cleanly:

Consent stateSubscribers% of cohortBraze-click rateMedian tagged sendsOpened anyBehavior (first principles)
Fully subscribed (0 off)2,533,91989.04%0.25%1576%Baseline — still receiving all promo streams
Partially opted out (1-5 off)42,8791.51%1.82%1289%Surgical / relevance-driven — turned off some streams, kept others; where most Braze email clicks land
Fully opted out (6 off)269,1159.46%0.56%271%Wholesale exit — quick, low-engagement; mostly non-Braze paths (preference center / GPC / global)
  • Partial = surgical / relevance-driven, and this is where Braze clicks concentrate. The partial bucket's Braze-click rate is 3.2× the fully-opted-out rate and 7.3× the fully-subscribed rate — plus the highest engagement of any bucket. People trim one stream (usually offer) and stay; 6,308 Braze clickers didn't even turn off a single category.
  • Full = quick, low-engagement wholesale exit. Median of only 2 tagged sends before leaving (vs 12 for partial) and the lowest open rate — a fast "stop all promo" decision through preference-center / global paths, not a single-email click. Only 322 (3.7% of Braze clickers) appear in both.

So: partial opt-outs are category-targeted and Braze-correlated; full opt-outs are quick, low-engagement, and mostly non-Braze. That difference in behavior is the mechanism behind the tiny overlap — the two signals measure different things, not the same thing twice. See the Signal Reconciliation tab for the full elimination funnels.

Live from prelim_consent_partial_vs_full.sql (current per-category state reconstructed from tax_stg_ca.stg_catax_email_consent; 2,845,913-subscriber FY26 mailed cohort). Braze-click rate = subscribers in the bucket with a FY26 Braze unsubscribe ÷ bucket size.

Journey Segmentation — Emails Before Full Opt-out

SegmentFull Opt-outs% of Opt-outsMedian Journey Length
Short (<=3 sends)41,68739.51
Medium (4-9 sends)37,76235.86
Long (10+ sends)26,14524.812

This is the distribution of tracked sends received before the consent transition. Per-email-count rates are intentionally not shown because a valid rate requires numerator and denominator to use the same stopping rule.

Ranked by attributed full opt-outs (last delivery within 90 days). Inferred exposure, not proven cause.

Appendix — Consent Draft Methodology & Diagnostics

Appendix: Attribution coverage & sensitivity
  • Exact FY26 full opt-outs: 105,594 (attributed 82,671 / unattributed 22,923).
  • Attribution coverage: 78.3% within the 90-day window.
  • Lookback sensitivity: 68.1% within 30 days · 76.4% within 60 days · 78.3% within 90 days.
  • Delivery-to-opt-out lag: median 4 days · p90 36 days.
  • Braze / consent overlap: 322 subscribers (3.7% of Braze unsubscribers) — this pipeline figure supersedes the earlier read-only exploration estimate of 324 (refined dedup + sha2 email grain).
  • Integrity checks: 0 post-opt-out leakage rows · 0 outside-90-day rows · 0 duplicate subscribers.
Appendix: Full opt-out by journey length
Journey LengthFull Opt-outs% of Opt-outs

4310.00
Appendix: Method — how the consent signal is defined
  • Outcome: first FY26 transition from fewer than six categories off to all six consent categories (product, offer, training, content, survey, partner) off, reconstructed from tax_stg_ca.stg_catax_email_consent change history. Never uses email_consent_summary.
  • Attribution: latest PRE/POST/ENG/DSKT delivery strictly before the opt-out and within 90 days (last-touch proxy). No qualifying delivery = Unattributed.
  • Saw before opt-out: % of full opt-outs who received that code as a send before opting out (journey-frequency, mirrors the Braze tab).
  • Cohort: subscribers who received a PRE/POST/ENG/DSKT send in FY26 (same denominator as the Braze tab).
  • Mode A vs Mode B (definitional lever): Mode A = all six categories off right now (undated current-state snapshot, ~258,696). Mode B = the first all-six-off transition inside FY26 (this tab, {optout_n:,}). We report Mode B because it is window-comparable to the Braze click rate. Never uses email_consent_summary.
  • Braze grain tie-out: the {braze_n:,} Braze figure is Tab 1's {total_unsubs:,} FY26 unsubscribers restricted to the tagged cohort (−{total_unsubs - int(pd.read_parquet(BASE / "cohort_unsub_emails.parquet")["unsubs"].sum()):,} with no PRE/POST/ENG/DSKT send) then reconciled from email-key to email-string grain. Full funnel on the Signal Reconciliation tab.
  • Not causal: the attributed campaign is the last email seen, not a proven cause of the opt-out. Use directionally for FY27 calendar planning.
Signal reconciliation. Tab 1 counts 11,733 Braze unsub clicks; Tab 2 counts 105,594 full promotional opt-outs. This tab shows, step by step, how each number is built, why they barely overlap, and which one to use — every identity below is machine-checked (13/13 pass, converged in 2 loop(s)).

Two Signals, Two Source Systems

SignalMechanismSource lineageGrainWindowWhat it captures
Braze unsubscribe clickClicked the unsubscribe link on one emailsrc_braze_tto_ca_email_unsubscribe -> fact_catax_mktg_event (event_type='unsubscribe')email / catax_email_keyFY26 events (Aug 1 2025 - Jul 31 2026)One-email / one-category intent (usually partial)
Derived full promotional opt-outAll six consent categories turned offstg_catax_email_consent change history (never email_consent_summary)email (lower(trim(email_address_str)))First all-6-off transition inside FY26Full suppression via any path — preference center, GPC token, CSR, global unsubscribe

The signals share the 2,845,735-subscriber FY26 mailed cohort as a denominator, but measure different exit mechanisms.

Braze Cohort Funnel — 11,733 → 8,607

All FY26 Braze unsubscribes start 11,733
Tab 1 headline; email-key grain
No PRE/POST/ENG/DSKT send in FY26 -3,153 8,580
Left via untagged mail (YRE / survey / churn) or zero sends — excluded by the tagged-cohort filter
Grain: email-key -> email-string +27 8,607
Tab 2 dedupes on lower(trim(email)); reconciles the two grains

How Tab 1's full Braze count reduces to the tagged-cohort figure Tab 2 compares against. The +27 step reconciles email-key vs email-string grain, so Tab 1 and Tab 2 provably tie out.

Where the excluded 3,174 went (fresh warehouse classification): 1,005 unsubscribed on a YRE newsletter, 1,920 on other untagged mail, and 242 on survey/churn campaigns — none had a PRE/POST/ENG/DSKT send, which is exactly why the tagged-cohort filter drops them.

Consent Opt-out Funnel — Mode A → 105,594

Current-state full opt-outs (all 6 off now) start 258,696
Mode A snapshot — undated; offline explore constant
First transition inside FY26 window -153,102 105,594
Mode B — the FY26-comparable full opt-out figure Tab 2 reports

Mode A = subscribers with all six categories off right now (undated snapshot). Mode B = the first all-six-off transition inside FY26 — the window-comparable figure Tab 2 reports. Of those, 82,671 are attributed to a last-touch delivery within 90 days and 22,923 are Unattributed.

Cross-signal Venn — Why 8,607 and 105,594 Barely Overlap

8,285
Braze-only
322
Both
105,272
Consent-only

Universe = the shared 8,607-Braze / 105,594-opt-out tagged cohort. Overlap is just 322; only 96 of them clicked Braze before fully opting out. The two circles are almost disjoint — they capture different behaviors.

Why the overlap is tiny (first principles)

Braze unsub clicks and full promotional opt-outs are two different exit doors. Three forces push their intersection (322 of a 113,879-person union) toward zero:

  1. Intent granularity. A Braze click is a single-email, single-category action — it usually turns off just the stream that annoyed the person (typically offer), leaving the other 5 on. Full opt-out needs all six off, so a click mechanically lands in the partial state, not full.
  2. Different exit doors. Full opt-outs mostly arrive via preference center / GPC token / CSR / global suppression — bulk actions that flip all six at once and emit no Braze email event.
  3. Base-rate asymmetry. 105,594 vs 8,607 (~12×). Even under independence the expected overlap is small; because the two are also mechanistically different, the observed 322 is far below even that.

The 96 who clicked Braze before the full opt-out are the only genuine "email click was a step toward total exit" cases; the rest clicked after or independently.

Which signal to use.
  • Campaign attribution ("which email drove the exit?") → use Braze events.
  • Full suppression / deliverability / treatment side-effect ("did cadence push people to kill all promo?") → use consent history.
  • Never compare the two rates head-to-head (0.30% vs 3.71%) as if they were the same metric — they answer different questions.

Attribution-window sensitivity

Of the 105,594 full opt-outs, the share with a tracked PRE/POST/ENG/DSKT delivery before the opt-out grows with the lookback:

30 days
68.1%
60 days
76.4%
90 days
78.3%

Median delivery-to-opt-out lag 4 days · p90 36 days. The 90-day window (the Tab 2 default) leaves 22% Unattributed.

Machine-checked Reconciliation

Self-correction loops

  • Loop 1 — fixes applied: [none] → failed: braze_terminal_matches_venn_universe, consent_terminal_matches_full_optouts
  • Loop 2 — fixes applied: [grain_align, window_align] → all pass

13 / 13 invariants pass. Tab 3 is generated only when every identity reconciles.

StatusCheckDetail
PASSbraze_funnel_reconcilesrunning totals equal cumulative deltas
PASSconsent_funnel_reconcilesrunning totals equal cumulative deltas
PASSbraze_terminal_matches_venn_universebraze funnel ends at 8,607; venn universe 8,607
PASSconsent_terminal_matches_full_optoutsconsent funnel ends at 105,594; full opt-outs 105,594
PASSvenn_braze_partitionbraze_only + overlap == braze universe
PASSvenn_consent_partitionconsent_only + overlap == opt-out universe
PASSvenn_union_sumsthree disjoint parts sum to the union
PASSoverlap_within_boundsoverlap cannot exceed either circle
PASSbraze_before_optout_within_overlapclicked-before subset is inside the overlap
PASSattribution_splitsattributed + unattributed == full opt-outs
PASSbraze_rate_recalculatesBraze rate == braze / cohort
PASSoptout_rate_recalculatesopt-out rate == opt-outs / cohort
PASScoverage_monotone68.1 <= 76.4 <= 78.3

Data Lineage

Appendix: Data lineage — how the tables are built & sourced

Layer metadata from the 2026-07-09 schema probe; view join SQL is live SHOW CREATE TABLE output (full text in lineage_ddl.sql), and our-analysis snippets are verbatim from prelim_*.sql.

Braze unsubscribe click

LayerTableGrainPartitionBuild & fields
Source (raw)tax_src_ca.src_braze_tto_ca_email_unsubscribeOne raw Braze unsubscribe export rowdateRaw Braze export landed in the data lake (24 cols).
Dropped downstream: dispatch_id, timezone, message_variation_id/name, canvas fields, send_id, ip_pool
Mart (fact)tax_dm_ca.fact_catax_mktg_eventOne marketing event (send / delivery / open / click / unsubscribe)tax_season_year_nbrPlatform ETL conforms raw Braze events into an event fact (PARQUET on S3), keyed to dim_catax_email, dim_catax_mktg_campaign and dim_catax_user_account. Populated by an upstream job — no view SQL; DDL shown.
13 cols incl. mktg_event_type_str, mktg_event_datetime, catax_email_key, catax_mktg_campaign_key
Report (view)tax_rpt_ca.v_rpt_email_marketing_eventsOne marketing event + campaign names(inherits fact)View: fact left-joined to the campaign dimension to attach campaign_name / campaign_step_name.
Adds campaign_name, campaign_step_name
Our analysisprelim_rates.sql / prelim_*.sqlDistinct catax_email_key (then lower(trim(email)))FY26 window filterJoin fact to the campaign dim, restrict to FY26, tag PRE/POST/ENG/DSKT, then count distinct unsubscribers.
Produces the 11,733 -> 8,607 Braze funnel

How each table is built (join SQL per hop):

→ tax_dm_ca.fact_catax_mktg_event
CREATE TABLE tax_dm_ca.fact_catax_mktg_event (
  catax_mktg_event_key STRING, source_system_key STRING,
  catax_mktg_campaign_key STRING, catax_user_account_key STRING,
  catax_email_key STRING, mktg_event_id STRING,
  mktg_event_type_str STRING, mktg_user_id STRING,
  mktg_external_user_id STRING, mktg_event_datetime STRING,
  dwh_creation_time TIMESTAMP, dwh_update_time TIMESTAMP,
  tax_season_year_nbr INT)
USING PARQUET PARTITIONED BY (tax_season_year_nbr);
-- rows populated by the upstream Braze ingestion job
→ tax_rpt_ca.v_rpt_email_marketing_events
CREATE VIEW tax_rpt_ca.v_rpt_email_marketing_events AS
select a.*, b.campaign_name, b.campaign_step_name
from tax_dm_ca.fact_catax_mktg_event a
left join tax_dm_ca.dim_catax_mktg_campaign b
  on a.catax_mktg_campaign_key = b.catax_mktg_campaign_key
→ prelim_rates.sql / prelim_*.sql
FROM tax_dm_ca.fact_catax_mktg_event e
JOIN tax_dm_ca.dim_catax_mktg_campaign c
  ON e.catax_mktg_campaign_key = c.catax_mktg_campaign_key
WHERE e.tax_season_year_nbr IN (2024, 2025)
  AND cast(e.mktg_event_datetime AS date)
      BETWEEN date '2025-08-01' AND date '2026-07-31'
-- unsubs = count(DISTINCT CASE WHEN lower(mktg_event_type_str)
--            = 'unsubscribe' THEN catax_email_key END)

Derived full promotional opt-out

LayerTableGrainPartitionBuild & fields
Source (history)tax_stg_ca.stg_catax_email_consentConsent snapshot rows; per-category true/false datetimesload_dateChange history of per-category consent (product, offer, training, content, survey, partner) with true/false timestamps. This is the working source for our analysis.
12 per-category *_true_datetime / *_false_datetime + email_address_str
Identity + GPCtax_dm_ca.dim_catax_email + common_dm.gpc_unsub_tokensOne row per email addressn/adim_catax_email supplies catax_email_key / email identity; gpc_unsub_tokens is only a GPC preference-center token bridge (no categories). Richer GPC content table unknown.
dim: catax_email_key, email_address_str, email_external_id; gpc: email_address, unsub_token, ingest_date
Mart (view)tax_dm_ca.v_email_consentOne row per email (current-state)n/aView: MAX(false/true) per category per email (consent_stg) -> category off if false > true (consent_stg2) -> email_consent_summary from the sum of category indicators -> dim_catax_email LEFT JOIN gpc LEFT JOIN consent. We BYPASS this view (and email_consent_summary); this role also lacks SELECT on it.
6 category flags + email_consent_summary (All Subscribed / Partially Subscribed / All Unsubscribed)
Our analysisprelim_consent_attribution.sql / prelim_consent_partial_vs_full.sqlOne row per email (event-time state or current state)FY26 windowWe rebuild category state directly from stg (same MAX false/true, off-if-false>true rule the view uses) — never email_consent_summary. Attribution: unpivot to a change timeline, carry running state, take the first all-6-off transition inside FY26 (Mode B, 105,594). Partial-vs-full: current-state category count per email (Mode A).
Produces 105,594 full opt-outs + the partial/full buckets

How each table is built (join SQL per hop):

→ tax_dm_ca.v_email_consent
CREATE VIEW tax_dm_ca.v_email_consent AS
WITH consent_stg AS (
  SELECT email_address_str,
         MAX(consent_product_false_datetime) AS product_f,
         MAX(consent_product_true_datetime)  AS product_t, ...
  FROM tax_stg_ca.stg_catax_email_consent GROUP BY email_address_str),
consent_stg2 AS (  -- category off if false > true
  SELECT ..., CASE WHEN COALESCE(product_f,'1900-01-01')
               > COALESCE(product_t,'1900-01-01')
               THEN 'Unsubscribed' ELSE 'Subscribed' END ...)
SELECT dce.catax_email_key, dce.email_address_str AS email,
       gpc.unsub_token AS gpc_unsub_token, consent.*
FROM tax_dm_ca.dim_catax_email dce
LEFT JOIN common_dm.gpc_unsub_tokens gpc
  ON gpc.email_address = dce.email_address_str
LEFT JOIN consent ON consent.email_address_str = dce.email_address_str
-- full DDL in lineage_ddl.sql
→ prelim_consent_attribution.sql / prelim_consent_partial_vs_full.sql
-- prelim_consent_attribution.sql (event-time state)
FROM tax_stg_ca.stg_catax_email_consent s
JOIN prelim_emails p ON lower(trim(s.email_address_str)) = p.email
LATERAL VIEW stack(12,
  'product', to_timestamp(s.consent_product_false_datetime), 0,
  'product', to_timestamp(s.consent_product_true_datetime),  1, ...)
  change AS category, change_dt, new_state
-- running state via last_value() over (partition by email
--   order by change_dt); first row where categories_off = 6
--   and categories_off_before < 6, inside the FY26 window.

-- prelim_consent_partial_vs_full.sql (current state)
-- off if max(false) IS NOT NULL AND (max(true) IS NULL
--   OR max(false) > max(true)); bucket by count of categories off
FY27 Strategy - campaign and customer-segment view. Unsubscribes are FY26 new full promotional opt-outs (official consent logic; gpc_unsub_token dropped) unioned with Braze non-QC Desktop, attributed to the latest campaign received before opt-out. Recommendations use Canvas / Campaign Name. Every number is traceable in the appendix at the bottom. Consent opt-out + Braze (non-QC Desktop)

The date range + phase drive the headline snapshot, the Season cards (volume by event date, Unsubs by opt-out date) and the Subscription Base (latest snapshot + trend within the window). The Campaign Deep-Dive is scoped by phase (not the date range) to keep each campaign's unsub counts accurate. Age/SKU/Tenure cuts have their own selectors in the sections below. Cells under 10 are suppressed.

FY26 Email Volume - Headline Snapshot

-Sent
-Delivered
-Opened
-OR
-Clicked
-CTOR
-Unsubs
-Unsub Rate

Order: Sent → Delivered → Open → OR (Open/Delivered) → Clicked → CTOR (Clicks/Opens) → Unsubs → Unsub Rate (Unsubs/Opened). Opens are directional (Apple MPP).

Season View

Phases match planning around NETFILE: Pre-Season Nov 1 - Feb 22; Season Feb 23 (official CRA NETFILE open) - Apr 30; Post-Season May 1 - Oct 31, 2026. Aug-Oct 2025 is outside these FY26 strategy phases and is not included in the three cards.

Subscription Base (by pseudonym)

Month-end promotional consent state per pseudonym: fully subscribed, partially subscribed, or fully unsubscribed, plus per-category counts. Reconstructed from consent history using the official v_email_consent rule. The status cards reflect the latest snapshot within the global date range / phase; the chart trends the fully-unsubscribed share across the months in that window.

FY27 Campaign Deep-Dive

Attribution coverage: 95,958 of 108,831 exits (88.2%) attribute to a Canvas Name (latest campaign received). The rest had no prior delivery and are Unattributed.

Canvas / Campaign NameOpensUnsubsUnsub/OpenReview

Review set is dynamic (not a fixed Top 5): ≥25 unsubs and rate ≥1.5x overall, or ≥5% of unsubs.

Unsubscribes by Segment - Time of Year and Day

FY26 unsubscribes by segment across the season phases, with the peak time-of-day bucket. Time-of-day uses broad warehouse-time buckets (raw Braze timezone is dropped upstream).

Frequency Before Unsubscribe - Segment Insight

Distinct tagged sends before the opt-out (exiters) vs before FY26 end (non-exiters), by segment. Observational association, not proof of causation.

Appendix - sources, SQL and definitions

Appendix A: Source ERD (backtrack any number)
TableGrainJoin key(s)Role
tax_stg_ca.stg_catax_email_consentemail x consent-historyemail_address_strConsent source of truth (12 category false/true datetimes). gpc_unsub_token not used.
tax_dm_ca.fact_catax_mktg_eventeventcatax_email_key, catax_mktg_campaign_keySend/Delivered/Open/Click/Unsubscribe events + timestamps.
tax_dm_ca.dim_catax_mktg_campaigncampaign/stepcatax_mktg_campaign_keyCanvas/Campaign Name and step (recommendation grain).
tax_dm_ca.dim_catax_emailemailcatax_email_key <-> email_address_strBridges events to email address.
tax_rpt_ca.rpt_accountpseudonymemail_address_str, pseudonym_id, province_nameIdentity + province (QC). Official email_consent_summary cross-check.
tax_rpt_ca.rpt_account_product_masterpseudonym x tax yearpseudonym_id (year=2025)Age, SKU (product family), tenure L2, service segment.

Flow: consent (stg_catax_email_consent) defines opt-out; events (fact_catax_mktg_event + dim_catax_mktg_campaign via dim_catax_email) supply volume and the latest-received Canvas Name; rpt_account (+ product master) supplies pseudonym, province/QC and segments.

Appendix B: Key-metric SQL (verbatim)
Flow full opt-out + Braze non-QC Desktop (attribution) (tab4_01_attr.sql)
-- Auto-composed from the shared Construct-B exit-set prefix. Do not hand-edit.

WITH marketing_events AS (
  SELECT
    lower(trim(em.email_address_str)) AS email,
    c.campaign_name,
    c.campaign_step_name,
    lower(e.mktg_event_type_str) AS event_type,
    CASE
      WHEN lower(e.mktg_event_type_str) IN ('delivery', 'open', 'click')
        THEN max(e.mktg_event_datetime)
      ELSE min(e.mktg_event_datetime)
    END AS event_dt
  FROM tax_dm_ca.fact_catax_mktg_event e
  JOIN tax_dm_ca.dim_catax_mktg_campaign c
    ON e.catax_mktg_campaign_key = c.catax_mktg_campaign_key
  JOIN tax_dm_ca.dim_catax_email em
    ON e.catax_email_key = em.catax_email_key
  WHERE e.tax_season_year_nbr IN (2024, 2025)
    AND cast(e.mktg_event_datetime AS date)
      BETWEEN date '2025-05-03' AND date '2026-07-31'
    AND em.email_address_str IS NOT NULL
  GROUP BY 1, 2, 3, 4
),
tagged_events AS (
  SELECT
    *,
    CASE
      WHEN campaign_step_name RLIKE '(^|-)PRE(-|$)'
        OR campaign_step_name RLIKE '(^|-)POST(-|$)'
        OR lower(campaign_step_name) LIKE '%emailengagement%'
        OR lower(campaign_step_name) LIKE '%desktop%'
      THEN 1 ELSE 0
    END AS flg_tagged,
    CASE WHEN lower(coalesce(campaign_step_name, '')) RLIKE 'desktop|dkt'
      THEN 1 ELSE 0 END AS flg_desktop,
    CASE WHEN lower(concat_ws(' ', campaign_name, campaign_step_name))
      RLIKE '(^|[^a-z])qc([^a-z]|$)|quebec' THEN 1 ELSE 0 END AS flg_qc_campaign
  FROM marketing_events
),
prelim_emails AS (
  SELECT DISTINCT email
  FROM tagged_events
  WHERE event_type = 'send'
    AND cast(event_dt AS date) >= date '2025-08-01'
    AND flg_tagged = 1
),
consent_agg AS (
  SELECT
    lower(trim(s.email_address_str)) AS email,
    max(to_timestamp(s.consent_product_false_datetime))  AS pf,
    max(to_timestamp(s.consent_product_true_datetime))   AS pt,
    max(to_timestamp(s.consent_offer_false_datetime))    AS ofa,
    max(to_timestamp(s.consent_offer_true_datetime))     AS ot,
    max(to_timestamp(s.consent_training_false_datetime)) AS tf,
    max(to_timestamp(s.consent_training_true_datetime))  AS tt,
    max(to_timestamp(s.consent_content_false_datetime))  AS cf,
    max(to_timestamp(s.consent_content_true_datetime))   AS ct,
    max(to_timestamp(s.consent_survey_false_datetime))   AS sf,
    max(to_timestamp(s.consent_survey_true_datetime))    AS st,
    max(to_timestamp(s.consent_partner_false_datetime))  AS pnf,
    max(to_timestamp(s.consent_partner_true_datetime))   AS pnt,
    max(CASE WHEN to_timestamp(s.consent_product_false_datetime)  <= timestamp '2025-07-31 23:59:59' THEN to_timestamp(s.consent_product_false_datetime)  END) AS pf0,
    max(CASE WHEN to_timestamp(s.consent_product_true_datetime)   <= timestamp '2025-07-31 23:59:59' THEN to_timestamp(s.consent_product_true_datetime)   END) AS pt0,
    max(CASE WHEN to_timestamp(s.consent_offer_false_datetime)    <= timestamp '2025-07-31 23:59:59' THEN to_timestamp(s.consent_offer_false_datetime)    END) AS ofa0,
    max(CASE WHEN to_timestamp(s.consent_offer_true_datetime)     <= timestamp '2025-07-31 23:59:59' THEN to_timestamp(s.consent_offer_true_datetime)     END) AS ot0,
    max(CASE WHEN to_timestamp(s.consent_training_false_datetime) <= timestamp '2025-07-31 23:59:59' THEN to_timestamp(s.consent_training_false_datetime) END) AS tf0,
    max(CASE WHEN to_timestamp(s.consent_training_true_datetime)  <= timestamp '2025-07-31 23:59:59' THEN to_timestamp(s.consent_training_true_datetime)  END) AS tt0,
    max(CASE WHEN to_timestamp(s.consent_content_false_datetime)  <= timestamp '2025-07-31 23:59:59' THEN to_timestamp(s.consent_content_false_datetime)  END) AS cf0,
    max(CASE WHEN to_timestamp(s.consent_content_true_datetime)   <= timestamp '2025-07-31 23:59:59' THEN to_timestamp(s.consent_content_true_datetime)   END) AS ct0,
    max(CASE WHEN to_timestamp(s.consent_survey_false_datetime)   <= timestamp '2025-07-31 23:59:59' THEN to_timestamp(s.consent_survey_false_datetime)   END) AS sf0,
    max(CASE WHEN to_timestamp(s.consent_survey_true_datetime)    <= timestamp '2025-07-31 23:59:59' THEN to_timestamp(s.consent_survey_true_datetime)    END) AS st0,
    max(CASE WHEN to_timestamp(s.consent_partner_false_datetime)  <= timestamp '2025-07-31 23:59:59' THEN to_timestamp(s.consent_partner_false_datetime)  END) AS pnf0,
    max(CASE WHEN to_timestamp(s.consent_partner_true_datetime)   <= timestamp '2025-07-31 23:59:59' THEN to_timestamp(s.consent_partner_true_datetime)   END) AS pnt0
  FROM tax_stg_ca.stg_catax_email_consent s
  JOIN prelim_emails p ON lower(trim(s.email_address_str)) = p.email
  WHERE s.email_address_str IS NOT NULL
  GROUP BY 1
),
consent_state AS (
  SELECT email,
    (CASE WHEN coalesce(pf, timestamp '1900-01-01')  > coalesce(pt, timestamp '1900-01-01')  THEN 1 ELSE 0 END
     + CASE WHEN coalesce(ofa,timestamp '1900-01-01') > coalesce(ot, timestamp '1900-01-01')  THEN 1 ELSE 0 END
     + CASE WHEN coalesce(tf, timestamp '1900-01-01') > coalesce(tt, timestamp '1900-01-01')  THEN 1 ELSE 0 END
     + CASE WHEN coalesce(cf, timestamp '1900-01-01') > coalesce(ct, timestamp '1900-01-01')  THEN 1 ELSE 0 END
     + CASE WHEN coalesce(sf, timestamp '1900-01-01') > coalesce(st, timestamp '1900-01-01')  THEN 1 ELSE 0 END
     + CASE WHEN coalesce(pnf,timestamp '1900-01-01') > coalesce(pnt,timestamp '1900-01-01')  THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) AS off_now,
    (CASE WHEN coalesce(pf0, timestamp '1900-01-01')  > coalesce(pt0, timestamp '1900-01-01')  THEN 1 ELSE 0 END
     + CASE WHEN coalesce(ofa0,timestamp '1900-01-01') > coalesce(ot0, timestamp '1900-01-01')  THEN 1 ELSE 0 END
     + CASE WHEN coalesce(tf0, timestamp '1900-01-01') > coalesce(tt0, timestamp '1900-01-01')  THEN 1 ELSE 0 END
     + CASE WHEN coalesce(cf0, timestamp '1900-01-01') > coalesce(ct0, timestamp '1900-01-01')  THEN 1 ELSE 0 END
     + CASE WHEN coalesce(sf0, timestamp '1900-01-01') > coalesce(st0, timestamp '1900-01-01')  THEN 1 ELSE 0 END
     + CASE WHEN coalesce(pnf0,timestamp '1900-01-01') > coalesce(pnt0,timestamp '1900-01-01')  THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) AS off_start,
    greatest(pf, ofa, tf, cf, sf, pnf) AS full_optout_dt
  FROM consent_agg
),
consent_exits AS (
  SELECT email, cast(full_optout_dt AS timestamp) AS exit_dt,
    'CONSENT' AS exit_source,
    cast(null AS string) AS b_campaign_name,
    cast(null AS string) AS b_campaign_step_name
  FROM consent_state
  WHERE off_now = 6 AND off_start < 6
    AND cast(full_optout_dt AS date) BETWEEN date '2025-08-01' AND date '2026-07-31'
),
email_province AS (
  SELECT email, province_name FROM (
    SELECT lower(trim(email_address_str)) AS email, province_name,
      row_number() OVER (PARTITION BY lower(trim(email_address_str)) ORDER BY updated_ts DESC NULLS LAST) AS rn
    FROM tax_rpt_ca.rpt_account
    WHERE email_address_str IS NOT NULL AND trim(email_address_str) <> ''
  ) r WHERE rn = 1
),
braze_desktop AS (
  SELECT email, cast(exit_dt AS timestamp) AS exit_dt, 'BRAZE_DESKTOP' AS exit_source,
    campaign_name AS b_campaign_name, campaign_step_name AS b_campaign_step_name
  FROM (
    SELECT t.email, t.event_dt AS exit_dt, t.campaign_name, t.campaign_step_name,
      row_number() OVER (PARTITION BY t.email ORDER BY t.event_dt, t.campaign_name, t.campaign_step_name) AS rn
    FROM tagged_events t
    JOIN prelim_emails p ON t.email = p.email
    LEFT JOIN email_province ep ON t.email = ep.email
    WHERE t.event_type = 'unsubscribe'
      AND cast(t.event_dt AS date) BETWEEN date '2025-08-01' AND date '2026-07-31'
      AND t.flg_desktop = 1 AND t.flg_qc_campaign = 0
      AND lower(coalesce(ep.province_name, '')) NOT IN ('quebec', 'qc', 'québec')
  ) x WHERE rn = 1
),
exit_union AS (
  SELECT * FROM consent_exits
  UNION ALL
  SELECT * FROM braze_desktop
),
deduped AS (
  SELECT email, exit_dt, exit_source, b_campaign_name, b_campaign_step_name
  FROM (
    SELECT u.*, row_number() OVER (
      PARTITION BY email
      ORDER BY CASE WHEN exit_source = 'CONSENT' THEN 0 ELSE 1 END, exit_dt,
               b_campaign_name ASC NULLS LAST, b_campaign_step_name ASC NULLS LAST
    ) AS rn
    FROM exit_union u
  ) y WHERE rn = 1
),
consent_rows AS (SELECT email, exit_dt FROM deduped WHERE exit_source = 'CONSENT'),
braze_rows AS (
  SELECT email, exit_dt, b_campaign_name, b_campaign_step_name FROM deduped WHERE exit_source = 'BRAZE_DESKTOP'
),
attr_ranked AS (
  SELECT cr.email, cr.exit_dt, e.campaign_name, e.campaign_step_name,
    e.event_dt AS received_dt, e.flg_desktop,
    row_number() OVER (
      PARTITION BY cr.email
      ORDER BY CASE WHEN e.event_type = 'delivery' THEN 0 ELSE 1 END,
               e.event_dt DESC, e.campaign_name, e.campaign_step_name
    ) AS rn
  FROM consent_rows cr
  LEFT JOIN tagged_events e
    ON cr.email = e.email AND e.event_type IN ('delivery', 'send') AND e.event_dt < cr.exit_dt
),
consent_attr AS (
  SELECT email, exit_dt, 'CONSENT' AS exit_source, campaign_name, campaign_step_name,
    CASE WHEN received_dt IS NULL THEN 'Unattributed' ELSE 'Latest received' END AS attribution_status,
    CASE WHEN received_dt IS NULL THEN cast(null AS int)
         ELSE datediff(cast(exit_dt AS date), cast(received_dt AS date)) END AS lag_days,
    CASE WHEN flg_desktop = 1 THEN true ELSE false END AS is_desktop
  FROM attr_ranked WHERE rn = 1
),
braze_attr AS (
  SELECT email, exit_dt, 'BRAZE_DESKTOP' AS exit_source, b_campaign_name AS campaign_name,
    b_campaign_step_name AS campaign_step_name, 'Direct desktop unsub' AS attribution_status,
    cast(null AS int) AS lag_days, true AS is_desktop
  FROM braze_rows
),
final AS (SELECT * FROM consent_attr UNION ALL SELECT * FROM braze_attr)
SELECT
  sha2(email, 256) AS subscriber_id,
  exit_dt, exit_source,
  'CONSENT_FLOW_PLUS_BRAZE_DESKTOP' AS source_mode,
  campaign_name, campaign_step_name, attribution_status, lag_days, is_desktop,
  CASE
    WHEN cast(exit_dt AS date) BETWEEN date '2025-11-01' AND date '2026-02-22' THEN 'Pre-Season'
    WHEN cast(exit_dt AS date) BETWEEN date '2026-02-23' AND date '2026-04-30' THEN 'Season'
    WHEN cast(exit_dt AS date) BETWEEN date '2026-05-01' AND date '2026-10-31' THEN 'Post-Season'
    ELSE 'Outside FY26 Strategy Phases'
  END AS phase,
  CASE
    WHEN hour(exit_dt) < 6 THEN '00:00-05:59'
    WHEN hour(exit_dt) < 12 THEN '06:00-11:59'
    WHEN hour(exit_dt) < 18 THEN '12:00-17:59'
    ELSE '18:00-23:59'
  END AS hour_bucket,
  CASE WHEN exit_source = 'CONSENT' THEN 'HIGH' ELSE 'MEDIUM' END AS confidence
FROM final
Sends before exit (frequency numerator) (tab4_01_sends.sql)
-- Auto-composed from the shared Construct-B exit-set prefix. Do not hand-edit.

WITH marketing_events AS (
  SELECT
    lower(trim(em.email_address_str)) AS email,
    c.campaign_name,
    c.campaign_step_name,
    lower(e.mktg_event_type_str) AS event_type,
    CASE
      WHEN lower(e.mktg_event_type_str) IN ('delivery', 'open', 'click')
        THEN max(e.mktg_event_datetime)
      ELSE min(e.mktg_event_datetime)
    END AS event_dt
  FROM tax_dm_ca.fact_catax_mktg_event e
  JOIN tax_dm_ca.dim_catax_mktg_campaign c
    ON e.catax_mktg_campaign_key = c.catax_mktg_campaign_key
  JOIN tax_dm_ca.dim_catax_email em
    ON e.catax_email_key = em.catax_email_key
  WHERE e.tax_season_year_nbr IN (2024, 2025)
    AND cast(e.mktg_event_datetime AS date)
      BETWEEN date '2025-05-03' AND date '2026-07-31'
    AND em.email_address_str IS NOT NULL
  GROUP BY 1, 2, 3, 4
),
tagged_events AS (
  SELECT
    *,
    CASE
      WHEN campaign_step_name RLIKE '(^|-)PRE(-|$)'
        OR campaign_step_name RLIKE '(^|-)POST(-|$)'
        OR lower(campaign_step_name) LIKE '%emailengagement%'
        OR lower(campaign_step_name) LIKE '%desktop%'
      THEN 1 ELSE 0
    END AS flg_tagged,
    CASE WHEN lower(coalesce(campaign_step_name, '')) RLIKE 'desktop|dkt'
      THEN 1 ELSE 0 END AS flg_desktop,
    CASE WHEN lower(concat_ws(' ', campaign_name, campaign_step_name))
      RLIKE '(^|[^a-z])qc([^a-z]|$)|quebec' THEN 1 ELSE 0 END AS flg_qc_campaign
  FROM marketing_events
),
prelim_emails AS (
  SELECT DISTINCT email
  FROM tagged_events
  WHERE event_type = 'send'
    AND cast(event_dt AS date) >= date '2025-08-01'
    AND flg_tagged = 1
),
consent_agg AS (
  SELECT
    lower(trim(s.email_address_str)) AS email,
    max(to_timestamp(s.consent_product_false_datetime))  AS pf,
    max(to_timestamp(s.consent_product_true_datetime))   AS pt,
    max(to_timestamp(s.consent_offer_false_datetime))    AS ofa,
    max(to_timestamp(s.consent_offer_true_datetime))     AS ot,
    max(to_timestamp(s.consent_training_false_datetime)) AS tf,
    max(to_timestamp(s.consent_training_true_datetime))  AS tt,
    max(to_timestamp(s.consent_content_false_datetime))  AS cf,
    max(to_timestamp(s.consent_content_true_datetime))   AS ct,
    max(to_timestamp(s.consent_survey_false_datetime))   AS sf,
    max(to_timestamp(s.consent_survey_true_datetime))    AS st,
    max(to_timestamp(s.consent_partner_false_datetime))  AS pnf,
    max(to_timestamp(s.consent_partner_true_datetime))   AS pnt,
    max(CASE WHEN to_timestamp(s.consent_product_false_datetime)  <= timestamp '2025-07-31 23:59:59' THEN to_timestamp(s.consent_product_false_datetime)  END) AS pf0,
    max(CASE WHEN to_timestamp(s.consent_product_true_datetime)   <= timestamp '2025-07-31 23:59:59' THEN to_timestamp(s.consent_product_true_datetime)   END) AS pt0,
    max(CASE WHEN to_timestamp(s.consent_offer_false_datetime)    <= timestamp '2025-07-31 23:59:59' THEN to_timestamp(s.consent_offer_false_datetime)    END) AS ofa0,
    max(CASE WHEN to_timestamp(s.consent_offer_true_datetime)     <= timestamp '2025-07-31 23:59:59' THEN to_timestamp(s.consent_offer_true_datetime)     END) AS ot0,
    max(CASE WHEN to_timestamp(s.consent_training_false_datetime) <= timestamp '2025-07-31 23:59:59' THEN to_timestamp(s.consent_training_false_datetime) END) AS tf0,
    max(CASE WHEN to_timestamp(s.consent_training_true_datetime)  <= timestamp '2025-07-31 23:59:59' THEN to_timestamp(s.consent_training_true_datetime)  END) AS tt0,
    max(CASE WHEN to_timestamp(s.consent_content_false_datetime)  <= timestamp '2025-07-31 23:59:59' THEN to_timestamp(s.consent_content_false_datetime)  END) AS cf0,
    max(CASE WHEN to_timestamp(s.consent_content_true_datetime)   <= timestamp '2025-07-31 23:59:59' THEN to_timestamp(s.consent_content_true_datetime)   END) AS ct0,
    max(CASE WHEN to_timestamp(s.consent_survey_false_datetime)   <= timestamp '2025-07-31 23:59:59' THEN to_timestamp(s.consent_survey_false_datetime)   END) AS sf0,
    max(CASE WHEN to_timestamp(s.consent_survey_true_datetime)    <= timestamp '2025-07-31 23:59:59' THEN to_timestamp(s.consent_survey_true_datetime)    END) AS st0,
    max(CASE WHEN to_timestamp(s.consent_partner_false_datetime)  <= timestamp '2025-07-31 23:59:59' THEN to_timestamp(s.consent_partner_false_datetime)  END) AS pnf0,
    max(CASE WHEN to_timestamp(s.consent_partner_true_datetime)   <= timestamp '2025-07-31 23:59:59' THEN to_timestamp(s.consent_partner_true_datetime)   END) AS pnt0
  FROM tax_stg_ca.stg_catax_email_consent s
  JOIN prelim_emails p ON lower(trim(s.email_address_str)) = p.email
  WHERE s.email_address_str IS NOT NULL
  GROUP BY 1
),
consent_state AS (
  SELECT email,
    (CASE WHEN coalesce(pf, timestamp '1900-01-01')  > coalesce(pt, timestamp '1900-01-01')  THEN 1 ELSE 0 END
     + CASE WHEN coalesce(ofa,timestamp '1900-01-01') > coalesce(ot, timestamp '1900-01-01')  THEN 1 ELSE 0 END
     + CASE WHEN coalesce(tf, timestamp '1900-01-01') > coalesce(tt, timestamp '1900-01-01')  THEN 1 ELSE 0 END
     + CASE WHEN coalesce(cf, timestamp '1900-01-01') > coalesce(ct, timestamp '1900-01-01')  THEN 1 ELSE 0 END
     + CASE WHEN coalesce(sf, timestamp '1900-01-01') > coalesce(st, timestamp '1900-01-01')  THEN 1 ELSE 0 END
     + CASE WHEN coalesce(pnf,timestamp '1900-01-01') > coalesce(pnt,timestamp '1900-01-01')  THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) AS off_now,
    (CASE WHEN coalesce(pf0, timestamp '1900-01-01')  > coalesce(pt0, timestamp '1900-01-01')  THEN 1 ELSE 0 END
     + CASE WHEN coalesce(ofa0,timestamp '1900-01-01') > coalesce(ot0, timestamp '1900-01-01')  THEN 1 ELSE 0 END
     + CASE WHEN coalesce(tf0, timestamp '1900-01-01') > coalesce(tt0, timestamp '1900-01-01')  THEN 1 ELSE 0 END
     + CASE WHEN coalesce(cf0, timestamp '1900-01-01') > coalesce(ct0, timestamp '1900-01-01')  THEN 1 ELSE 0 END
     + CASE WHEN coalesce(sf0, timestamp '1900-01-01') > coalesce(st0, timestamp '1900-01-01')  THEN 1 ELSE 0 END
     + CASE WHEN coalesce(pnf0,timestamp '1900-01-01') > coalesce(pnt0,timestamp '1900-01-01')  THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) AS off_start,
    greatest(pf, ofa, tf, cf, sf, pnf) AS full_optout_dt
  FROM consent_agg
),
consent_exits AS (
  SELECT email, cast(full_optout_dt AS timestamp) AS exit_dt,
    'CONSENT' AS exit_source,
    cast(null AS string) AS b_campaign_name,
    cast(null AS string) AS b_campaign_step_name
  FROM consent_state
  WHERE off_now = 6 AND off_start < 6
    AND cast(full_optout_dt AS date) BETWEEN date '2025-08-01' AND date '2026-07-31'
),
email_province AS (
  SELECT email, province_name FROM (
    SELECT lower(trim(email_address_str)) AS email, province_name,
      row_number() OVER (PARTITION BY lower(trim(email_address_str)) ORDER BY updated_ts DESC NULLS LAST) AS rn
    FROM tax_rpt_ca.rpt_account
    WHERE email_address_str IS NOT NULL AND trim(email_address_str) <> ''
  ) r WHERE rn = 1
),
braze_desktop AS (
  SELECT email, cast(exit_dt AS timestamp) AS exit_dt, 'BRAZE_DESKTOP' AS exit_source,
    campaign_name AS b_campaign_name, campaign_step_name AS b_campaign_step_name
  FROM (
    SELECT t.email, t.event_dt AS exit_dt, t.campaign_name, t.campaign_step_name,
      row_number() OVER (PARTITION BY t.email ORDER BY t.event_dt, t.campaign_name, t.campaign_step_name) AS rn
    FROM tagged_events t
    JOIN prelim_emails p ON t.email = p.email
    LEFT JOIN email_province ep ON t.email = ep.email
    WHERE t.event_type = 'unsubscribe'
      AND cast(t.event_dt AS date) BETWEEN date '2025-08-01' AND date '2026-07-31'
      AND t.flg_desktop = 1 AND t.flg_qc_campaign = 0
      AND lower(coalesce(ep.province_name, '')) NOT IN ('quebec', 'qc', 'québec')
  ) x WHERE rn = 1
),
exit_union AS (
  SELECT * FROM consent_exits
  UNION ALL
  SELECT * FROM braze_desktop
),
deduped AS (
  SELECT email, exit_dt, exit_source, b_campaign_name, b_campaign_step_name
  FROM (
    SELECT u.*, row_number() OVER (
      PARTITION BY email
      ORDER BY CASE WHEN exit_source = 'CONSENT' THEN 0 ELSE 1 END, exit_dt,
               b_campaign_name ASC NULLS LAST, b_campaign_step_name ASC NULLS LAST
    ) AS rn
    FROM exit_union u
  ) y WHERE rn = 1
),
sends_before AS (
  SELECT d.email,
    count(DISTINCT concat_ws('||', e.campaign_name, e.campaign_step_name)) AS sends_before
  FROM deduped d
  LEFT JOIN tagged_events e
    ON d.email = e.email AND e.event_type = 'send'
   AND cast(e.event_dt AS date) >= date '2025-08-01'
   AND e.event_dt < d.exit_dt AND e.flg_tagged = 1
  GROUP BY 1
)
SELECT
  sha2(email, 256) AS subscriber_id,
  sends_before,
  CASE WHEN sends_before <= 3 THEN '<=3' WHEN sends_before <= 9 THEN '4-9' ELSE '10+' END AS frequency_bucket
FROM sends_before
Segment / QC profile sidecar (tab4_01_profile.sql)
-- Tab 4 Construct-B profile sidecar: segment + QC per mailed-cohort email.
-- SKU: PAM (auth_id, most-recent year) then rpt_account_email_marketing by email.
-- Keyed by sha2(email) so it merges to the exit set in Python.

WITH sends AS (
  SELECT
    lower(trim(em.email_address_str)) AS email,
    c.campaign_step_name
  FROM tax_dm_ca.fact_catax_mktg_event e
  JOIN tax_dm_ca.dim_catax_mktg_campaign c
    ON e.catax_mktg_campaign_key = c.catax_mktg_campaign_key
  JOIN tax_dm_ca.dim_catax_email em
    ON e.catax_email_key = em.catax_email_key
  WHERE e.tax_season_year_nbr IN (2024, 2025)
    AND lower(e.mktg_event_type_str) = 'send'
    AND cast(e.mktg_event_datetime AS date) BETWEEN date '2025-08-01' AND date '2026-07-31'
    AND em.email_address_str IS NOT NULL
),
prelim_emails AS (
  SELECT DISTINCT email
  FROM sends
  WHERE campaign_step_name RLIKE '(^|-)PRE(-|$)'
     OR campaign_step_name RLIKE '(^|-)POST(-|$)'
     OR lower(campaign_step_name) LIKE '%emailengagement%'
     OR lower(campaign_step_name) LIKE '%desktop%'
),
pam_age AS (
  SELECT auth_id, age_nbr
  FROM (
    SELECT
      auth_id,
      age_nbr,
      row_number() OVER (
        PARTITION BY auth_id
        ORDER BY tax_product_year_nbr DESC NULLS LAST
      ) AS rn
    FROM tax_rpt_ca.rpt_account_product_master
    WHERE auth_id IS NOT NULL
  ) x WHERE rn = 1
),
aem AS (
  SELECT email, age_nbr, product_family_name, tenure_l2_name, product_category_name, product_group_name
  FROM (
    SELECT
      lower(trim(email_address_str)) AS email,
      age_nbr, product_family_name, tenure_l2_name, product_category_name, product_group_name,
      row_number() OVER (
        PARTITION BY lower(trim(email_address_str))
        ORDER BY tax_product_year_nbr DESC NULLS LAST
      ) AS rn
    FROM tax_rpt_ca.rpt_account_email_marketing
    WHERE email_address_str IS NOT NULL AND trim(email_address_str) <> ''
  ) x WHERE rn = 1
),
sin AS (
  SELECT auth_id, age_nbr
  FROM (
    SELECT
      auth_id,
      age_nbr,
      row_number() OVER (
        PARTITION BY auth_id
        ORDER BY tax_product_year_nbr DESC NULLS LAST
      ) AS rn
    FROM tax_rpt_ca.rpt_sin_master
    WHERE auth_id IS NOT NULL
  ) x WHERE rn = 1
),
profile AS (
  SELECT email, age_band, sku, tenure_l2, service_segment, province_name
  FROM (
    SELECT
      lower(trim(a.email_address_str)) AS email,
      a.province_name,
      CASE
        WHEN coalesce(
          try_cast(pam_age.age_nbr AS int),
          try_cast(aem.age_nbr AS int),
          try_cast(sin.age_nbr AS int)
        ) <= 25 THEN '25U'
        WHEN coalesce(
          try_cast(pam_age.age_nbr AS int),
          try_cast(aem.age_nbr AS int),
          try_cast(sin.age_nbr AS int)
        ) >= 26 THEN '26O'
        ELSE 'Unknown'
      END AS age_band,
      CASE
        WHEN nullif(trim(pam.current_product_family_name), '') IS NOT NULL
          THEN trim(pam.current_product_family_name)
        WHEN nullif(trim(aem.product_family_name), '') IS NOT NULL
          THEN trim(aem.product_family_name)
        WHEN pam.auth_id IS NULL AND aem.email IS NULL THEN 'Not in PAM'
        ELSE 'No product (never started)'
      END AS sku,
      coalesce(
        nullif(trim(pam.account_tenure_l2_name), ''),
        nullif(trim(aem.tenure_l2_name), ''),
        'Unknown'
      ) AS tenure_l2,
      CASE
        WHEN upper(coalesce(pam.current_product_category_name, aem.product_category_name, '')) = 'FS' THEN 'Full Service'
        WHEN upper(coalesce(pam.current_product_group_name, aem.product_group_name, '')) = 'ASSISTED'
          OR upper(coalesce(pam.current_product_category_name, aem.product_category_name, '')) = 'A&R' THEN 'Assisted'
        WHEN coalesce(try_cast(pam.se_income_ind AS int), 0) = 1
          OR upper(coalesce(pam.current_product_family_name, aem.product_family_name, '')) RLIKE '(^|[ &])SE($|[ &])' THEN 'SE'
        WHEN pam.auth_id IS NOT NULL OR aem.email IS NOT NULL THEN 'DIY'
        ELSE 'Unknown'
      END AS service_segment,
      row_number() OVER (
        PARTITION BY lower(trim(a.email_address_str))
        ORDER BY pam.tax_product_year_nbr DESC NULLS LAST, pam.updated_ts DESC NULLS LAST, a.updated_ts DESC NULLS LAST
      ) AS rn
    FROM tax_rpt_ca.rpt_account a
    LEFT JOIN tax_rpt_ca.rpt_account_product_master pam ON a.auth_id = pam.auth_id
    LEFT JOIN pam_age ON a.auth_id = pam_age.auth_id
    LEFT JOIN aem ON lower(trim(a.email_address_str)) = aem.email
    LEFT JOIN sin ON a.auth_id = sin.auth_id
    WHERE a.email_address_str IS NOT NULL AND trim(a.email_address_str) <> ''
  ) r
  WHERE rn = 1
)
SELECT
  sha2(p.email, 256) AS subscriber_id,
  coalesce(pr.age_band, 'Unknown') AS age_band,
  coalesce(pr.sku, 'Unknown') AS sku,
  coalesce(pr.tenure_l2, 'Unknown') AS tenure_l2,
  coalesce(pr.service_segment, 'Unknown') AS service_segment,
  CASE WHEN lower(coalesce(pr.province_name, '')) IN ('quebec', 'qc', 'québec')
    THEN 'QC' ELSE 'non-QC' END AS qc_status
FROM prelim_emails p
LEFT JOIN profile pr ON p.email = pr.email
Headline daily send volume (tab4_02_daily_volume.sql)
-- Tab 4 daily physical-send volume and engagement (send grain).
-- Sent / Delivered / Opened / Clicked only; unsubscribes are the flow exit
-- signal (tab4_unified_unsubs) and are attached by exit date / attributed
-- Canvas Name in Python, so this query carries no unsub join and stays light.
-- SKU/service overlay campaign Desktop and Biz Tax (not present in PAM).
-- Aggregate output only; no recipient identifier leaves the query.

WITH event_base AS (
  SELECT
    lower(trim(em.email_address_str)) AS email,
    coalesce(c.campaign_name, 'Unknown') AS campaign_name,
    coalesce(c.campaign_step_name, 'Unknown') AS campaign_step_name,
    lower(trim(e.mktg_event_type_str)) AS event_type,
    to_timestamp(e.mktg_event_datetime) AS event_ts
  FROM tax_dm_ca.fact_catax_mktg_event e
  JOIN tax_dm_ca.dim_catax_mktg_campaign c
    ON e.catax_mktg_campaign_key = c.catax_mktg_campaign_key
  JOIN tax_dm_ca.dim_catax_email em
    ON e.catax_email_key = em.catax_email_key
  WHERE e.tax_season_year_nbr IN (2024, 2025)
    AND cast(e.mktg_event_datetime AS date) BETWEEN date '2025-08-01' AND date '2026-07-31'
    AND em.email_address_str IS NOT NULL
),
pam_age AS (
  SELECT auth_id, age_nbr
  FROM (
    SELECT
      auth_id,
      age_nbr,
      row_number() OVER (
        PARTITION BY auth_id
        ORDER BY tax_product_year_nbr DESC NULLS LAST
      ) AS rn
    FROM tax_rpt_ca.rpt_account_product_master
    WHERE auth_id IS NOT NULL
  ) x WHERE rn = 1
),
aem AS (
  SELECT email, age_nbr, product_family_name, tenure_l2_name, product_category_name, product_group_name
  FROM (
    SELECT
      lower(trim(email_address_str)) AS email,
      age_nbr, product_family_name, tenure_l2_name, product_category_name, product_group_name,
      row_number() OVER (
        PARTITION BY lower(trim(email_address_str))
        ORDER BY tax_product_year_nbr DESC NULLS LAST
      ) AS rn
    FROM tax_rpt_ca.rpt_account_email_marketing
    WHERE email_address_str IS NOT NULL AND trim(email_address_str) <> ''
  ) x WHERE rn = 1
),
sin AS (
  SELECT auth_id, age_nbr
  FROM (
    SELECT
      auth_id,
      age_nbr,
      row_number() OVER (
        PARTITION BY auth_id
        ORDER BY tax_product_year_nbr DESC NULLS LAST
      ) AS rn
    FROM tax_rpt_ca.rpt_sin_master
    WHERE auth_id IS NOT NULL
  ) x WHERE rn = 1
),
recipient_profile AS (
  SELECT email, age_band, pam_sku, tenure_l2, pam_service
  FROM (
    SELECT
      lower(trim(a.email_address_str)) AS email,
      CASE
        WHEN coalesce(
          try_cast(pam_age.age_nbr AS int),
          try_cast(aem.age_nbr AS int),
          try_cast(sin.age_nbr AS int)
        ) <= 25 THEN '25U'
        WHEN coalesce(
          try_cast(pam_age.age_nbr AS int),
          try_cast(aem.age_nbr AS int),
          try_cast(sin.age_nbr AS int)
        ) >= 26 THEN '26O'
        ELSE 'Unknown'
      END AS age_band,
      CASE
        WHEN nullif(trim(pam.current_product_family_name), '') IS NOT NULL
          THEN trim(pam.current_product_family_name)
        WHEN nullif(trim(aem.product_family_name), '') IS NOT NULL
          THEN trim(aem.product_family_name)
        WHEN pam.auth_id IS NULL AND aem.email IS NULL THEN 'Not in PAM'
        ELSE 'No product (never started)'
      END AS pam_sku,
      coalesce(
        nullif(trim(pam.account_tenure_l2_name), ''),
        nullif(trim(aem.tenure_l2_name), ''),
        'Unknown'
      ) AS tenure_l2,
      CASE
        WHEN upper(coalesce(pam.current_product_category_name, aem.product_category_name, '')) = 'FS' THEN 'Full Service'
        WHEN upper(coalesce(pam.current_product_group_name, aem.product_group_name, '')) = 'ASSISTED'
          OR upper(coalesce(pam.current_product_category_name, aem.product_category_name, '')) = 'A&R' THEN 'Assisted'
        WHEN coalesce(try_cast(pam.se_income_ind AS int), 0) = 1
          OR upper(coalesce(pam.current_product_family_name, aem.product_family_name, '')) RLIKE '(^|[ &])SE($|[ &])' THEN 'SE'
        WHEN pam.auth_id IS NOT NULL OR aem.email IS NOT NULL THEN 'DIY'
        ELSE 'Unknown'
      END AS pam_service,
      row_number() OVER (
        PARTITION BY lower(trim(a.email_address_str))
        ORDER BY pam.tax_product_year_nbr DESC NULLS LAST, pam.updated_ts DESC NULLS LAST, a.updated_ts DESC NULLS LAST
      ) AS rn
    FROM tax_rpt_ca.rpt_account a
    LEFT JOIN tax_rpt_ca.rpt_account_product_master pam
      ON a.auth_id = pam.auth_id
    LEFT JOIN pam_age ON a.auth_id = pam_age.auth_id
    LEFT JOIN aem ON lower(trim(a.email_address_str)) = aem.email
    LEFT JOIN sin ON a.auth_id = sin.auth_id
    WHERE a.email_address_str IS NOT NULL AND trim(a.email_address_str) <> ''
  ) r
  WHERE rn = 1
),
physical_sends AS (
  SELECT
    email, campaign_name, campaign_step_name,
    min(CASE WHEN event_type = 'send' THEN event_ts END) AS send_ts,
    max(CASE WHEN event_type = 'delivery' THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) AS delivered_ind,
    max(CASE WHEN event_type = 'open' THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) AS opened_ind,
    max(CASE WHEN event_type = 'click' THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) AS clicked_ind
  FROM event_base
  GROUP BY 1, 2, 3
  HAVING min(CASE WHEN event_type = 'send' THEN event_ts END) IS NOT NULL
),
send_enriched AS (
  SELECT
    cast(p.send_ts AS date) AS event_date,
    p.campaign_name,
    p.campaign_step_name,
    p.delivered_ind, p.opened_ind, p.clicked_ind,
    CASE
      WHEN cast(p.send_ts AS date) BETWEEN date '2025-11-01' AND date '2026-02-22' THEN 'Pre-Season'
      WHEN cast(p.send_ts AS date) BETWEEN date '2026-02-23' AND date '2026-04-30' THEN 'Season'
      WHEN cast(p.send_ts AS date) BETWEEN date '2026-05-01' AND date '2026-10-31' THEN 'Post-Season'
      ELSE 'Outside FY26 Strategy Phases'
    END AS phase,
    coalesce(pr.age_band, 'Unknown') AS age_band,
    CASE
      WHEN lower(concat_ws(' ', p.campaign_name, p.campaign_step_name)) RLIKE 'desktop|dkt' THEN 'Desktop'
      WHEN lower(concat_ws(' ', p.campaign_name, p.campaign_step_name)) RLIKE 'biz[ _-]?tax|business[ _-]?tax' THEN 'Biz Tax'
      WHEN lower(concat_ws(' ', p.campaign_name, p.campaign_step_name)) RLIKE 'full[ _-]?service' THEN 'Full Service'
      WHEN lower(concat_ws(' ', p.campaign_name, p.campaign_step_name)) RLIKE 'self[ _-]?employed|(^|[^a-z])se([^a-z]|$)' THEN 'SE'
      ELSE coalesce(pr.pam_sku, 'Unknown')
    END AS sku,
    CASE
      WHEN lower(concat_ws(' ', p.campaign_name, p.campaign_step_name)) RLIKE 'desktop|dkt' THEN 'Desktop'
      WHEN lower(concat_ws(' ', p.campaign_name, p.campaign_step_name)) RLIKE 'assist(ed)?' THEN 'Assisted'
      WHEN lower(concat_ws(' ', p.campaign_name, p.campaign_step_name)) RLIKE 'full[ _-]?service' THEN 'Full Service'
      ELSE coalesce(pr.pam_service, 'Unknown')
    END AS service_segment,
    coalesce(pr.tenure_l2, 'Unknown') AS tenure_l2
  FROM physical_sends p
  LEFT JOIN recipient_profile pr ON p.email = pr.email
)
SELECT
  event_date, campaign_name, campaign_step_name, phase,
  age_band, sku, tenure_l2, service_segment,
  'CONSENT_FLOW_PLUS_BRAZE_DESKTOP' AS source_mode,
  count(*) AS sent,
  sum(delivered_ind) AS delivered,
  sum(opened_ind) AS opened,
  round(100.0 * sum(opened_ind) / nullif(sum(delivered_ind), 0), 4) AS or_pct,
  sum(clicked_ind) AS clicked,
  round(100.0 * sum(clicked_ind) / nullif(sum(opened_ind), 0), 4) AS ctor_pct
FROM send_enriched
GROUP BY
  event_date, campaign_name, campaign_step_name, phase, age_band, sku,
  tenure_l2, service_segment
ORDER BY event_date, campaign_name, campaign_step_name
Subscription base (Construct A) (tab4_04_subscription_snapshot.sql)
-- Tab 4 subscription base (Construct A): month-end consent state by pseudonym.
--
-- Official consent logic expressed as an event timeline: a category's state at
-- month-end T is the state (0=off,1=on) of the latest consent event with
-- change_dt <= T. off_count 0..6 -> Fully subscribed(0) / Partially(1-5) /
-- Fully unsubscribed(6). This equals v_email_consent's MAX(false)>MAX(true)
-- rule evaluated as of each month-end. gpc_unsub_token is not used.
--
-- Identity/segment: one pseudonym per rpt_account row, carrying its email;
-- consent is joined by that email. Segments come from rpt_account_product_master.
-- Grain: month x age x SKU x tenure x status x category. Aggregates only; no
-- identifier is exported.

WITH months(snapshot_month) AS (
  SELECT * FROM VALUES
    (date '2025-08-31'), (date '2025-09-30'), (date '2025-10-31'),
    (date '2025-11-30'), (date '2025-12-31'), (date '2026-01-31'),
    (date '2026-02-28'), (date '2026-03-31'), (date '2026-04-30'),
    (date '2026-05-31'), (date '2026-06-30'), (date '2026-07-31')
),
categories(category) AS (
  SELECT * FROM VALUES
    ('product'), ('offer'), ('training'), ('content'), ('survey'), ('partner')
),
-- Distinct emails that exist in the consent system (the email subscription
-- base). Restricting to these keeps the month x category timeline tractable;
-- accounts with no consent record are not part of the email base.
consent_email_list AS (
  SELECT DISTINCT lower(trim(email_address_str)) AS email
  FROM tax_stg_ca.stg_catax_email_consent
  WHERE email_address_str IS NOT NULL AND trim(email_address_str) <> ''
),
-- One pseudonym per account, with its email and profile segment.
pam_age AS (
  SELECT auth_id, age_nbr
  FROM (
    SELECT
      auth_id,
      age_nbr,
      row_number() OVER (
        PARTITION BY auth_id
        ORDER BY tax_product_year_nbr DESC NULLS LAST
      ) AS rn
    FROM tax_rpt_ca.rpt_account_product_master
    WHERE auth_id IS NOT NULL
  ) x WHERE rn = 1
),
aem AS (
  SELECT email, age_nbr, product_family_name, tenure_l2_name, product_category_name, product_group_name
  FROM (
    SELECT
      lower(trim(email_address_str)) AS email,
      age_nbr, product_family_name, tenure_l2_name, product_category_name, product_group_name,
      row_number() OVER (
        PARTITION BY lower(trim(email_address_str))
        ORDER BY tax_product_year_nbr DESC NULLS LAST
      ) AS rn
    FROM tax_rpt_ca.rpt_account_email_marketing
    WHERE email_address_str IS NOT NULL AND trim(email_address_str) <> ''
  ) x WHERE rn = 1
),
sin AS (
  SELECT auth_id, age_nbr
  FROM (
    SELECT
      auth_id,
      age_nbr,
      row_number() OVER (
        PARTITION BY auth_id
        ORDER BY tax_product_year_nbr DESC NULLS LAST
      ) AS rn
    FROM tax_rpt_ca.rpt_sin_master
    WHERE auth_id IS NOT NULL
  ) x WHERE rn = 1
),
account_base AS (
  SELECT pseudonym_id, email, age_band, sku, tenure_l2, service_segment
  FROM (
    SELECT
      trim(a.pseudonym_id) AS pseudonym_id,
      lower(trim(a.email_address_str)) AS email,
      CASE
        WHEN coalesce(
          try_cast(pam_age.age_nbr AS int),
          try_cast(aem.age_nbr AS int),
          try_cast(sin.age_nbr AS int)
        ) <= 25 THEN '25U'
        WHEN coalesce(
          try_cast(pam_age.age_nbr AS int),
          try_cast(aem.age_nbr AS int),
          try_cast(sin.age_nbr AS int)
        ) >= 26 THEN '26O'
        ELSE 'Unknown'
      END AS age_band,
      CASE
        WHEN nullif(trim(pam.current_product_family_name), '') IS NOT NULL
          THEN trim(pam.current_product_family_name)
        WHEN nullif(trim(aem.product_family_name), '') IS NOT NULL
          THEN trim(aem.product_family_name)
        WHEN pam.auth_id IS NULL AND aem.email IS NULL THEN 'Not in PAM'
        ELSE 'No product (never started)'
      END AS sku,
      coalesce(
        nullif(trim(pam.account_tenure_l2_name), ''),
        nullif(trim(aem.tenure_l2_name), ''),
        'Unknown'
      ) AS tenure_l2,
      CASE
        WHEN upper(coalesce(pam.current_product_category_name, aem.product_category_name, '')) = 'FS' THEN 'Full Service'
        WHEN upper(coalesce(pam.current_product_group_name, aem.product_group_name, '')) = 'ASSISTED'
          OR upper(coalesce(pam.current_product_category_name, aem.product_category_name, '')) = 'A&R' THEN 'Assisted'
        WHEN coalesce(try_cast(pam.se_income_ind AS int), 0) = 1
          OR upper(coalesce(pam.current_product_family_name, aem.product_family_name, '')) RLIKE '(^|[ &])SE($|[ &])' THEN 'SE'
        WHEN pam.auth_id IS NOT NULL OR aem.email IS NOT NULL THEN 'DIY'
        ELSE 'Unknown'
      END AS service_segment,
      row_number() OVER (
        PARTITION BY trim(a.pseudonym_id)
        ORDER BY pam.tax_product_year_nbr DESC NULLS LAST, pam.updated_ts DESC NULLS LAST, a.updated_ts DESC NULLS LAST
      ) AS rn
    FROM tax_rpt_ca.rpt_account a
    JOIN consent_email_list cel
      ON lower(trim(a.email_address_str)) = cel.email
    LEFT JOIN tax_rpt_ca.rpt_account_product_master pam
      ON a.auth_id = pam.auth_id
    LEFT JOIN pam_age ON a.auth_id = pam_age.auth_id
    LEFT JOIN aem ON lower(trim(a.email_address_str)) = aem.email
    LEFT JOIN sin ON a.auth_id = sin.auth_id
    WHERE a.pseudonym_id IS NOT NULL AND trim(a.pseudonym_id) <> ''
      AND a.email_address_str IS NOT NULL AND trim(a.email_address_str) <> ''
  ) r
  WHERE rn = 1
),
-- Consent events unpivoted to (email, category, change_dt, new_state).
consent_events AS (
  SELECT
    lower(trim(s.email_address_str)) AS email,
    change.category,
    change.change_dt,
    change.new_state
  FROM tax_stg_ca.stg_catax_email_consent s
  LATERAL VIEW stack(
    12,
    'product',  to_timestamp(s.consent_product_false_datetime),  0,
    'product',  to_timestamp(s.consent_product_true_datetime),   1,
    'offer',    to_timestamp(s.consent_offer_false_datetime),    0,
    'offer',    to_timestamp(s.consent_offer_true_datetime),     1,
    'training', to_timestamp(s.consent_training_false_datetime), 0,
    'training', to_timestamp(s.consent_training_true_datetime),  1,
    'content',  to_timestamp(s.consent_content_false_datetime),  0,
    'content',  to_timestamp(s.consent_content_true_datetime),   1,
    'survey',   to_timestamp(s.consent_survey_false_datetime),   0,
    'survey',   to_timestamp(s.consent_survey_true_datetime),    1,
    'partner',  to_timestamp(s.consent_partner_false_datetime),  0,
    'partner',  to_timestamp(s.consent_partner_true_datetime),   1
  ) change AS category, change_dt, new_state
  WHERE s.email_address_str IS NOT NULL
    AND change.change_dt IS NOT NULL
    AND cast(change.change_dt AS date) <= date '2026-07-31'
),
-- Restrict consent to emails that map to an account pseudonym.
consent_account AS (
  SELECT b.pseudonym_id, e.category, e.change_dt, e.new_state
  FROM consent_events e
  JOIN account_base b ON e.email = b.email
),
-- Per pseudonym x month x category: state = latest event <= month-end (else on).
pseudonym_month_category AS (
  SELECT
    b.pseudonym_id,
    m.snapshot_month,
    b.age_band, b.sku, b.tenure_l2, b.service_segment,
    c.category,
    coalesce(
      max_by(
        ca.new_state,
        named_struct('change_dt', ca.change_dt, 'new_state', ca.new_state)
      ),
      1
    ) AS subscribed_state
  FROM account_base b
  CROSS JOIN months m
  CROSS JOIN categories c
  LEFT JOIN consent_account ca
    ON b.pseudonym_id = ca.pseudonym_id
   AND c.category = ca.category
   AND ca.change_dt < date_add(m.snapshot_month, 1)
  GROUP BY 1, 2, 3, 4, 5, 6, 7
),
with_off_count AS (
  SELECT *,
    sum(CASE WHEN subscribed_state = 0 THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) OVER (
      PARTITION BY pseudonym_id, snapshot_month
    ) AS categories_off
  FROM pseudonym_month_category
),
labeled AS (
  SELECT
    snapshot_month, age_band, sku, tenure_l2, service_segment, category, subscribed_state, pseudonym_id,
    CASE
      WHEN categories_off = 0 THEN 'Fully subscribed'
      WHEN categories_off BETWEEN 1 AND 5 THEN 'Partially subscribed'
      WHEN categories_off = 6 THEN 'Fully unsubscribed'
      ELSE 'Invalid'
    END AS status
  FROM with_off_count
)
SELECT
  snapshot_month,
  age_band,
  sku,
  tenure_l2,
  service_segment,
  status,
  category,
  sum(CASE WHEN subscribed_state = 1 THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) AS subscribed_n,
  sum(CASE WHEN subscribed_state = 0 THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) AS unsubscribed_n,
  count(*) AS pseudonym_n
FROM labeled
WHERE status <> 'Invalid'
GROUP BY 1, 2, 3, 4, 5, 6, 7
Frequency cohort base (tab4_06_frequency.sql)
-- Tab 4 frequency cohort (denominator base).
--
-- This emits ONE row per mailed customer with their FY26 tagged-send exposure
-- and profile segment. It deliberately avoids the consent reconstruction and
-- per-segment self-unions: those, combined with the full-cohort distinct-send
-- computation, exceed the warehouse request timeout. The exiter numerator and
-- the exit "stop" come from the already-validated unified exit parquet, so the
-- final association table is assembled in build_frequency.py:
--   * non-exiter stop  = FY26 window end  -> sends_before_stop = total_sends
--   * exiter stop       = exit timestamp   -> sends_before comes from the
--                          unified exit parquet (sends strictly before exit)
-- This reproduces the original stopping-rule semantics deterministically.
--
-- Grain/privacy: one row per sha2(email); no raw identifier is exported.

WITH sends AS (
  SELECT
    lower(trim(em.email_address_str)) AS email,
    c.campaign_name,
    c.campaign_step_name,
    cast(e.mktg_event_datetime AS date) AS send_date
  FROM tax_dm_ca.fact_catax_mktg_event e
  JOIN tax_dm_ca.dim_catax_mktg_campaign c
    ON e.catax_mktg_campaign_key = c.catax_mktg_campaign_key
  JOIN tax_dm_ca.dim_catax_email em
    ON e.catax_email_key = em.catax_email_key
  WHERE e.tax_season_year_nbr IN (2024, 2025)
    AND lower(e.mktg_event_type_str) = 'send'
    AND cast(e.mktg_event_datetime AS date)
      BETWEEN date '2025-08-01' AND date '2026-07-31'
    AND em.email_address_str IS NOT NULL
),
tagged_sends AS (
  SELECT email, campaign_name, campaign_step_name, send_date
  FROM sends
  WHERE campaign_step_name RLIKE '(^|-)PRE(-|$)'
     OR campaign_step_name RLIKE '(^|-)POST(-|$)'
     OR lower(campaign_step_name) LIKE '%emailengagement%'
     OR lower(campaign_step_name) LIKE '%desktop%'
),
cohort AS (
  SELECT
    email,
    count(DISTINCT concat_ws('||', campaign_name, campaign_step_name)) AS total_sends,
    count(DISTINCT CASE
      WHEN send_date BETWEEN date '2025-11-01' AND date '2026-02-22'
      THEN concat_ws('||', campaign_name, campaign_step_name) END
    ) AS pre_sends,
    count(DISTINCT CASE
      WHEN send_date BETWEEN date '2026-02-23' AND date '2026-04-30'
      THEN concat_ws('||', campaign_name, campaign_step_name) END
    ) AS season_sends,
    count(DISTINCT CASE
      WHEN send_date BETWEEN date '2026-05-01' AND date '2026-10-31'
      THEN concat_ws('||', campaign_name, campaign_step_name) END
    ) AS post_sends,
    count(DISTINCT CASE WHEN date_format(send_date, 'yyyy-MM') = '2025-08' THEN concat_ws('||', campaign_name, campaign_step_name) END) AS m_2025_08,
    count(DISTINCT CASE WHEN date_format(send_date, 'yyyy-MM') = '2025-09' THEN concat_ws('||', campaign_name, campaign_step_name) END) AS m_2025_09,
    count(DISTINCT CASE WHEN date_format(send_date, 'yyyy-MM') = '2025-10' THEN concat_ws('||', campaign_name, campaign_step_name) END) AS m_2025_10,
    count(DISTINCT CASE WHEN date_format(send_date, 'yyyy-MM') = '2025-11' THEN concat_ws('||', campaign_name, campaign_step_name) END) AS m_2025_11,
    count(DISTINCT CASE WHEN date_format(send_date, 'yyyy-MM') = '2025-12' THEN concat_ws('||', campaign_name, campaign_step_name) END) AS m_2025_12,
    count(DISTINCT CASE WHEN date_format(send_date, 'yyyy-MM') = '2026-01' THEN concat_ws('||', campaign_name, campaign_step_name) END) AS m_2026_01,
    count(DISTINCT CASE WHEN date_format(send_date, 'yyyy-MM') = '2026-02' THEN concat_ws('||', campaign_name, campaign_step_name) END) AS m_2026_02,
    count(DISTINCT CASE WHEN date_format(send_date, 'yyyy-MM') = '2026-03' THEN concat_ws('||', campaign_name, campaign_step_name) END) AS m_2026_03,
    count(DISTINCT CASE WHEN date_format(send_date, 'yyyy-MM') = '2026-04' THEN concat_ws('||', campaign_name, campaign_step_name) END) AS m_2026_04,
    count(DISTINCT CASE WHEN date_format(send_date, 'yyyy-MM') = '2026-05' THEN concat_ws('||', campaign_name, campaign_step_name) END) AS m_2026_05,
    count(DISTINCT CASE WHEN date_format(send_date, 'yyyy-MM') = '2026-06' THEN concat_ws('||', campaign_name, campaign_step_name) END) AS m_2026_06,
    count(DISTINCT CASE WHEN date_format(send_date, 'yyyy-MM') = '2026-07' THEN concat_ws('||', campaign_name, campaign_step_name) END) AS m_2026_07
  FROM tagged_sends
  GROUP BY email
),
pam_age AS (
  SELECT auth_id, age_nbr
  FROM (
    SELECT
      auth_id,
      age_nbr,
      row_number() OVER (
        PARTITION BY auth_id
        ORDER BY tax_product_year_nbr DESC NULLS LAST
      ) AS rn
    FROM tax_rpt_ca.rpt_account_product_master
    WHERE auth_id IS NOT NULL
  ) x WHERE rn = 1
),
aem AS (
  SELECT email, age_nbr, product_family_name, tenure_l2_name, product_category_name, product_group_name
  FROM (
    SELECT
      lower(trim(email_address_str)) AS email,
      age_nbr, product_family_name, tenure_l2_name, product_category_name, product_group_name,
      row_number() OVER (
        PARTITION BY lower(trim(email_address_str))
        ORDER BY tax_product_year_nbr DESC NULLS LAST
      ) AS rn
    FROM tax_rpt_ca.rpt_account_email_marketing
    WHERE email_address_str IS NOT NULL AND trim(email_address_str) <> ''
  ) x WHERE rn = 1
),
sin AS (
  SELECT auth_id, age_nbr
  FROM (
    SELECT
      auth_id,
      age_nbr,
      row_number() OVER (
        PARTITION BY auth_id
        ORDER BY tax_product_year_nbr DESC NULLS LAST
      ) AS rn
    FROM tax_rpt_ca.rpt_sin_master
    WHERE auth_id IS NOT NULL
  ) x WHERE rn = 1
),
profile AS (
  SELECT email, age_band, sku, tenure_l2, service_segment
  FROM (
    SELECT
      lower(trim(em.email_address_str)) AS email,
      CASE
        WHEN coalesce(
          try_cast(pam_age.age_nbr AS int),
          try_cast(aem.age_nbr AS int),
          try_cast(sin.age_nbr AS int)
        ) <= 25 THEN '25U'
        WHEN coalesce(
          try_cast(pam_age.age_nbr AS int),
          try_cast(aem.age_nbr AS int),
          try_cast(sin.age_nbr AS int)
        ) >= 26 THEN '26O'
        ELSE 'Unknown'
